steps.title: Target the concern
steps.content: The concern was that virtual tours would make in-person visits seem unnecessary. To support the administrators, the evidence should show that the tours did not discourage in-person visits and may even have encouraged them.

steps.title: Choose direct support
steps.content: Choice A says visitors were unaware of the tours, so it does not show the tours' effect. Choice B gives location information only. Choice D describes post-visit reminiscing, not the decision to visit. Evidence that virtual tours convinced many people to plan in-person visits directly answers the concern. The answer is C.
